promoted_symbols: WP_Http::request proxy enabled and send-through decision
candidate.promoted_behavior: Only the proxy enabled plus send-through branch decision in WP_Http::request is emitted by generated Haxe PHP. WP_HTTP_Proxy policy, pre_http_send_through_proxy filtering, proxy object construction, authentication property handoff, Requests dispatch, and response handling remain PHP-owned.

cases.focus: configured proxy with authentication becomes a Requests Proxy\Http option with address and credentials
site host and WP_PROXY_BYPASS_HOSTS wildcard hosts bypass proxy option handoff
pre_http_send_through_proxy false/true overrides proxy option handoff for specific URLs

side_effect_policy.runtime_stubs: Requests classes, selected WordPress globals, hook dispatch, site URL, proxy constants, mbstring encoding guards, and option/bloginfo helpers are deterministic stubs. Copied WP_Http and HTTP support classes remain the executed request proxy option sources; outbound Requests::request records options without network I/O.

remaining_gaps.detail: Only the proxy enabled plus send-through branch decision is Haxe-owned. WP_HTTP_Proxy enablement, host/bypass matching, pre_http_send_through_proxy filtering, proxy address/authentication handoff, native Requests proxy object behavior, Requests dispatch, response filtering, and actual proxy negotiation remain PHP-owned or unclaimed in this candidate.

The fixture records WP_Http's Requests and proxy option handoff through a stubbed Requests::request boundary. It does not perform live network I/O, DNS, TLS, proxy negotiation, redirect following, or transport execution.
The fixture uses PHP CLI with deterministic support stubs rather than an installed WordPress distribution or ecosystem HTTP callers.
The fixture compares copied oracle PHP in both roots; generated original-path PHP replacement remains a later cross-domain gate.
